message_text:
You budgeted for the mortgage. You accounted for taxes and insurance. But the average single-family homeowner spends an additional $21,400 per year on utilities, maintenance, and other operating costs, according to Bankrate's 2025 study. In high-cost states, it can hit $29,000.

It's not just the amount — it's the surprise. 42% of homeowners told Bankrate that hidden maintenance costs were their single biggest regret about buying.

The thing is, much of this is predictable if you know where to look. The age and efficiency of the HVAC system, the condition of the water heater, insulation levels, appliance efficiency — these are the drivers. They're just not always visible in a listing.

Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar makes them visible. It estimates what a home will cost to run and how it compares to similar homes nearby — so whether you're buying or evaluating your own home, you can plan with real numbers instead of hoping for the best.

Look up any home at pearlscore.com.

Two homes at the same price can cost very different amounts to actually live in. The difference comes down to things you won't see in listing photos — the age and efficiency of the HVAC system, insulation quality, water heater condition, and appliance performance.

Bankrate's 2025 study found the average homeowner spends $21,400/year beyond the mortgage. But that's an average — and the gap between an efficient home and an inefficient one can add up to thousands of dollars every year.

The good news? These cost drivers are measurable. And homes that run efficiently have a real advantage that's been hard to see until now. Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar estimates what a home will cost to run and compares it to similar properties nearby — so you can evaluate total cost of ownership before you make an offer.

Look up any home at pearlscore.com.
